calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Instructing Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Rothstein appeared by telephone for Union Bank (Petitioner/Trustee).
Anthony Gantner appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that Howard Slavitt is not present. Mr. Rothstein stated that he has not heard from Mr. Slavitt, that Mr. Slavitt's Client has not taken a position on one issue, that Mr. Slavitt got notice of the continued hearing date and that it was his impression that Mr. Slavitt would not appear today. Mr. Gantner stated that he did not know if Mr. Slavitt will be appearing, or not. Mr. Rothstein stated that there is about $1,100,000.00 left in residue, that the question is how the residue is to be distributed, that the rest has been distributed to John, and that there is a question about constructing and interpretation of the trust document, and the power of appointment. Mr. Gantner stated that he does not know if it is accurate to say that there is $1,200,000.00 in assets that are supposed to go to the named charities and that there is some income. Mr. Rothstein stated that all the residue goes to the charitable beneficiaries, that he has consents from four out of six charities to the position taken by Union Bank, that he does believe an evidentiary hearing is necessary and that he thinks everything is already before the Court. Mr. Gantner stated that he waives having an evidentiary hearing and submits on the papers. Mr. Gantner also provided history/background, stated that the trust required monthly distribution to John Erskine, that John did not take any distributions for 30-years and that the income should not be part of that trust. In addition, Mr. Gantner stated that it is easy to determine interest versus income, and that non-interest income should not be going to the beneficiaries. Mr. Rothstein spoke of the intent of the trust and the exercise of power of appointment of Mr. Erskine. The Court noted that the other issues in the petition are resolved and withdrawn, and asked Mr. Rothstein to provide a clean form of order that does not deal with the proceeds from the property. The Petition for Order Instructing Trustee is under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-30-2020 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Instructing Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-30-2020 Continued To Jun-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Instructing Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Continued To Mar-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Rothstein for MUFG Union Bank (Trustee).
Mitchell Edwards for John Gantner (Beneficiary/Respondent).

The Court noted that Anthony Gantner is not present and questions what the Trustee's view is regarding the relevant standard for capacity. Mr. Rothstein stated that he is not sure Anthony has raised the issue properly, that he will have a real problem proving his position and that there are prior exercises of power of appointment. The Court noted that Mr. Rothstein is the petitioner and raised this issue, questions why the Parties are here, that the Trustee's got to make hard decisions sometimes and that Court can't step into the Trustee's shoes. Mr. Edwards raised the issue of Anthony not being here and stated that Anthony did not request an evidentiary hearing. The Petition for Order Instructing Trustee is continued to March 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Anthony one more shot. The Parties are to provide a joint status report by March 23, 2020 indicating the agreed upon facts relative to this petition, what the disputed material facts are, who the witnesses will be, and time estimates of witness testimony, and why there doesn't need to be an evidentiary between Mr. Edwards and Anthony. An Order to Appear will be issued to Anthony Gantner to appear on March 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear and ordering him to participate in preparation of the joint status report for submission by March 23, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.

Petition For Order Instructing Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-07-2020 Continued To Jan-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Instructing Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-24-2019 Continued To Jan-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Instructing Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Continued To Dec-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Gary D. Rothstein for MUFG Union Bank, N.A. (Petitioner/Trustee). Howard Slavitt for John Gantner (Respondent). Heather Olinto for SF Planning and Urban Research Association (Interested Party).

The Court inquired if Mr. Rothstein had received the probate examiner notes. Mr. Rothstein confirmed for the Court that he had received the notes. Mr. Rothstein stated that the Trustee can come in for a petition for instructions and that they can seek the Court's instruction. Mr. Rothstein addressed the impartiality that's imposed on the Trustee, stated that the Trustee must present both sides equally, that the Trustee must be neutral, and that the beneficiaries disagree. Mr. Slavitt submitted on their response with regard as to what the Court should do. The Court addressed Mr. Rothstein and inquired if he believed that they had "flushed out A and B". Mr. Rothstein stated that he believed that they had, that it doesn't matter to them, but that it does matter to Anthony, et al. The Court addressed Mr. Rothstein stating that he was asking the Court to do too much, that the Court needs to respond to a specific proposal, and that the best way to resolve it is for the Trustee to take a position. Mr. Rothstein stated that the Trustee will take a position. The Court also stated that the Trustee should do the best that they can to act and that if any interested party disagrees that they can come in and tell the Court why. The matter is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.
